title: Temeke Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002
title_en: Temeke Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This Instrument may be cited as the Temeke Municipal Council ( Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002 and shall come into operation on the date of its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Instrument shall apply throughout the jurisdiction of the Temeke Municipal Council .

In the Instrument unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Local Government (Urban authorities) Act. 1982; " Health Centre Governing Committee " means a committee established under section 18 of this Instrument; " Health Centre management Team " means a team of head of section or units of a Health Centre ; " Ward Health Committee " means a committee established under Section 15 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act ; " Dispensary Governing Committee " means a committee established under section 39(1) of the Instrument: " Council Health Management Team " means a technical health managerial organ of the Municipality established under section 13 of this Instrument; " Dispensary " means a primary health care facility which offers out patients services, including Reproductive and child health services, promotive services which includes environmental health, community based health care, school health and information, education and communication services and may have up to four beds for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours; " District " means and includes a city, municipal or town; " Health Centre " means a health facility usually located at the Ward level offering outpatient and inpatient health care services, reproductive and child health services, promotive services which includes environmen­tal health, community based health care, school health and information, education and communication services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds. " Hospital " means a level 1 hospital headed by a Medical officer in charge, which answers to the population health needs by delivering second line quality, preventive, promotive, curative and rehabilitative health care services which include in patient services, outpatient services, supportive services (which include radiology, laboratory/blood bank, pharmacy theatre and mortuary services) and administrative services such as material, financial and human resources; " Medical Officer of Health " (MMOH) means a medical practitioner who is the Officer in charge of health services in the Municipal. " Health Facility " means and includes a hospital, health centre, medical and dental clinics, dispensary, pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnostic centre, maternity home nursing home; and such other facilities established or organized for the purposes of delivering health services; " Public health facility " means a health facility that is mainly owned and managed by the Government to address peoples needs; " Private Health facility " means health facility owned and managed by an individual or corporate body. " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Local Government and Regional Administration; " Non Government Organization (NGO) Health Facility " means a health facility owned, financed and run by an organization or an association normally is charitable non profit making with the aim of complimenting public or private services and operates within laid down regulations of the Government; " Board " means the Council Health Service Board established under section 4(1) of this Instrument; " Council " means the Municipal Council of Temeke established under section 5 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act . 1982; " Regional Health Management Team " means a managerial organ established by the Ministry of health; [ Act No. 8 of 1982 ]

provisions.title: Establishment of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a Board to be known as the Municipal Council Health Service Board ; (2) The Offices of the Board shall be within the Temeke Municipal Council .

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be composed of the following members namely— (a) Elected members (vote members) (i) Four community service users of Public health facility of whom at least two shall be female; (ii) One representative from NGO's or voluntary agency and professional associations (iii) The Chairman of the Council Social Services Committee. (b) Secretariat; (i) The Municipal Economist; (ii) The Medical Officer of Health who shall be the Secretary to the Board ; (iii) One representative from the Hospital health board or Medical Officer In charge of the District Hospital (iv) One representative from the Regional Secretariet. (2) The Chairman shall be elected from amongst members specified under paragraphs (a) , of this section; (3) Members specified under paragraphs (a) other than members specified under paragraphs (b) , shall have voting rights in any decision of the Board .

provisions.title: Tenure of office and mode of selection
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of their nomination and cease to be a member after expiry of that period, but shall be eligible for renomination for another term of three years. (2) At the end of the tenure of office of the current Board the Council Director shall announce to the public the open vacancy, which require competent personnel to fill them by filling special application form and submit them to the Council . (3) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by the reason of death, resignation, permanent incapacity or any other reason, another person shall be nominated by the Board to fill up the vacancy and a person nominated shall hold office for the remaining term, provided that, nothing in this section shall be construed as allowing to nominate another person if the remaining period is less than six months. (4) The person so nominated as per subsection 3 above and 2 above is eligible for re-election. (5) Selection of applicants shall be coordinated by the council Health management Team and there after be forwarded to the full Council through Economic and social services committee. (6) The Board shall be approved by the full Council and inaugurated under the witness of the District Commissioner. If the board fails to fulfill its expected obligations as stipulated by the relevant council's Instrument, the Council shall reprimand it and may dissolve the board.

provisions.title: Meetings of council health service Board
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of subsection (2) , of this section, the Board shall ordinarily meet quarterly in every calendar year and the Chairman shall convene all meetings of the Board. (2) The chairman may convene a special or an extra-ordinary meeting of the Board on request in writing signed by not less than one third of the total number of members of the Board when the need arises.

provisions.title: Quorum and voting at meeting
provisions.content: (1) One-half of elected members shall constitute a quorum at any meeting of the Board : (2) A decision of the majority members present at a Board meeting shall be deemed to be a decision of the Board . (3) Secret ballots system shall be used in Board 's meetings. (4) Every member of the Board shall have one vote and in event of equal votes, the Chairman of the meeting shall have a casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members
provisions.content: (1) The qualification for Board members shall base on three aspects as provided hereunder:— (a) shall be a Tanzanian a citizen (b) for representative of NGO'S and Voluntary Agency, the question of citizenship shall not apply. (c) he has attained the age of 25 years and not older than seventy years, provided that the subject is of sound mind. (d) applicants shall have at least completed secondary education and above. (2) A Councilor or an employee of the Council , whether elected or not save for chairman, Economic and social committee shall not allow to be a voting member of the Board .

provisions.title: Roles and functions of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The roles, function and responsibilities of the Board shall be as follows:— (a) to ensure that the population receives appropriate and affordable, promotive, preventive, curative and rehabilitative health care services; (b) to discuss, amend health plans, budget and submit to the Council for approval; (c) to receive, analyse and approve implementation reports from the Council Health Management Team ; (d) to identify, mobilize and solicit financial resources for running council health services; (e) to mobilise adequate resources that will ensure effective efficient and equitable access to health services in the Municipality; (f) to support Council Health management Team in managing and administering health resources; (g) to prepare and recommend on the council wide health plan and budget the committee responsible for health services; (h) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable logistics and supply system; (i) to develop, supervise and manage health services in Municipality and; (j) to advise and recommend the Municipal on human resources recruitment, training selection and employment.

provisions.title: Relationship of the Board with community stakeholders and other authorities
provisions.content: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities shall be as follows:— (a) The Board shall ensure full involvement of the local community in priority identification, planning, financial contributions, monitoring, and evaluation of health services delivery. (b) The Board shall be accountable to the Council whereby:— (i) The Council Health Plans, quarterly, annual technical and financial reports shall be reviewed by Economic and Social services Committee and the finance and administration Committee and approved by full Council . (ii) The Board shall conduct its activities in such a way that it shall maximize the efficiency of the Council and its existing organs without the Council ’s jeopardizing the Board 's autonomy. (c) The Council Health Management Team shall be a technical structure for implementing decisions passed by the Board . (d) In respect of Economic and Social Services Committee:— (i) All plans and progress reports shall pass; therefore, this committee will facilitate relationship. (ii) Close collaboration with other sectors will be actively searched by the Board towards the implementation of joint efforts in priority areas, within the Council . (e) With Non-Governmental Organizations, private providers and donors:— (i) Members representing the Voluntary Agency, Non Governmental Organizations and private providers in the Board will facilitate the relationship. (ii) The Board Will require a proof from Council Health Management Team that other stakeholders have been involved in the planning process; (iii) The Board shall solicit funds from various sources including Councils, Voluntary Agency, Community and donors; (f) The Regional Health Management Team shall provide technical support and advisory role; (g) The Ministries responsible for Regional Administration and Local Government and Health shall give technical support to the Board by formulating policies regulatory framework and monitoring of the Board 's activities;

provisions.title: Sources and Management of Funds
provisions.content: (1) The sources of funds for the Board shall be:— (a) Community contributions; (b) Cost sharing and user charges fees, i.e. Bamako initiative approach (c) Council revenue collection; (d) Donors and partners; (e) Central Government grants; (f) Other sources legally acquired. (2) There shall be a single health services account to be known as account number 6. The Management of this account shall follow Local Government financial Memorandum and regulations. Signatories shall be as prescribed from time to time. (3) The Board shall maintain fund account for the purpose of Health fund initiatives. This account will maintain revenues and collection from user fees from council health facilities. The account will be used to contribute resources to account No. 6 of the council in financing health services. Signatory of this special account will be as stipulated in the by-law established health funds. (4) The Council shall appoint two accountants who shall oversee health accounts under the Medical Officer of Health . (5) The Medical Officer of Health shall initiate and originate all payments in respect of account No. 6. (6) The account shall be audited following the prevailing Government regulations. (7) By-laws for health funds shall be formed for the purpose of management of its activities in that particular area.

provisions.title: Establishment of Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established in the district, the Council Health Management Team .

provisions.title: Composition of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: (1) The Council Health Management Team shall consist of:— (a) Municipal Medical Officer of Health ; (b) Municipal Health Planner; (c) Municipal Health Secretary; (d) Municipal Program Coordinator; (e) Medical Officer in charge of Municipal Hospital ; (f) Medical Officer I/c of Health center. (g) Clinical Officers I/c of Dispensaries on rotational basis every quarter. (2) Any of the following team can be invited to attend the Municipal Council Health Management Committee. (i) Under Planning section Research Coordinator; Health Management Information System (HMIS); Continue Education Coordinator. (ii) Under Administration Maintenance team leader Supplies Officer Accountant (iii) Under Programmes (a) Preventive program. Reproductive and child health Disease/vector/vermin Control and EPI Coordinator Malaria Control Coordinator TB/Leprosy Coordinator STD/HIV AIDS Coordinator (b) Curative Program OPD/1PD Coordinator Drugs Coordinator Radiologist Clinical Laboratory Technician Dentist Ophthalmology Psychiatry (c) Promotive Program Environmental Health Officer Community Based Health Care Coordinator Information Education Communication Coordinator School Health Program Coordinator (d) Private Secretariat OPD/IPD Coordinator Reproductive and Child health Coordinator Social Service Coordinator.

provisions.title: Roles and Functions of the Municipal Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Roles and function of the Municipal Health Management Team shall be:— (a) To prepare comprehensive Municipal health plans in line with the Municipal health needs and National Health Planning Guidelines; (b) To ensure that hospitals, health centers and dispensaries implement health activities community owned resources persons and the communities as per comprehensive Municipal health plan; (c) To ensure the provision of transport, drugs, vaccines, medical supplies, equipment and reagents to hospitals, health centres and dispensaries. (d) To respond to epidemics, emergencies and plan for control and preventive measures accordingly: (e) To carry out supportive supervision to Municipal health staff at all levels in the Municipality: (f) To ensure that quality health services are provided in line with national standards: (g) To ensure health legislation, regulation and professional ethics, and codes of conduct are adhered to in the Municipality; (h) To ensure that data collection, analysis, utilization and feedback are used by health workers to plan and implement proper intervention; (i) To identify priority areas organise and conduct operational research in the Municipality; (j) To compile and analyse quarterly and annually technical and all financial reports and submit them to the Council Health Service Board and to the Regional Health Management Team ; (k) To organise meeting of all key health partners operating in the Municipality in order to identify and coordinate all health activities; (l) To organise and support outreach health services; (m) To monitor and evaluate implementation of health activities in the Municipality.

provisions.title: Establishment of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established for each hospital under the management and supervision of the council a committee to be referred to as a Hospital Governing Committee.

provisions.title: Composition of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: The Hospital Governing Committee shall consist of: (a) Elected members (i) Three persons of reputable character appointed from among; the members of the community receiving health services or registered to receive services from the hospital of which at least one should be a female. (ii) Two persons appointed by the Council from members of the Health Centre Committee and dispensary Committee within the area of the Municipality; (iii) One person appointed by the Council from voluntary agencies providing services within the Council ; (b) Secretariat (i) One person from the Council Health board; (ii) Medical Officer in-charge of the Council hospital who shall be a secretary; (iii) One representative from the office of the Medical Officer of Health . (2) At least one third of the members shall be women. (3) Hospital Governing Committee may invite persons who are not their members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the Committee;

provisions.title: Qualifications of members of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: A person shall qualify for appointment as member of Hospital Governing Committee if he is:— (a) a citizen of the United Republic of Tanzania. (b) 25 years of age and above and of sound mind (c) Secondary school leaver or above and can read and write in Kiswahili and English. (d) not holding any political post.

provisions.title: Procedure for selecting Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of section (2) all members of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be selected in accordance with principles of meritocracy and transparency. (2) The position of membership to the Hospital Governing Committee shall be advertised in the newspaper circulating in the area of jurisdiction of the Council and such advertisement shall call upon qualified person to submit application to the Council Director; through the WEO of the area of residence. (3) Selection of elected candidates shall be made by full council through the Economic and Social Services Committee. (4) The Chairman of the committee shall be selected amongst elected members.

provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) The Tenure of office of a member of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be three years and at the end of that period a member can be eligible for re-nomination for another term of three years only; (2) If the Hospital Governing Committee fails to fulfill its expected obligations the Council shall reprimand or dissolve the committee and arrange for an immediate election.

provisions.title: Meetings of the Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital Governing Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the transaction of its business after every 3 months at places and dates and such times as the Committee may decide. (2) The Chairman of the Hospital Governing Committee may after consultation with the Secretary of the Committee convene an extra­ordinary meeting on such time as it may deem necessary. (3) Notice of every ordinary meeting shall be sent to the members not less than two days before the date appointed for the meeting, notifying members in writing of the place, date, and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: The functions, roles and responsibilities of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be: (a) to oversee proper management of resources of the hospital. (b) to Receive, discuss and pass proposals for hospital plan and budgets and submit to the Council for approval through Council Health Services Board . (c) to receive and discuss the implementation reports prepared by Hospital management Team. (d) to identify mobilize and solicit financial resources for the financing of the hospital services. (e) to liaise with other health committees and partners in health provision and promotion. (f) to make recommendations on selection, employment training and deployment to the relevant body of the Council so as to ensure proper, effective, and efficient human resource disposition of the hospital. (g) to provide feed back to community on Hospital operations and development plans.

provisions.title: Funds and sources of revenue
provisions.content: (1) The sources of revenue for the Hospital governing committee shall constitute of: (a) such money that shall be allocated to the Hospital in the councils budget and appropriated by the Council by way of Grant through council health service board; (b) health service user charges or part of such charges assigned by the council health service board to form part of resources of the hospital; (c) such amount which may be received by way of grant, subvention, donations from the Council & Central government, institutions or private individuals; and (d) any other money that may lawfully be received by the board from any other sources not specified in this instrument. (2) There should be a single hospital account, which shall follow prevailing local government financial regulations. (3) The Municipal medical Officer will assign one of his accountants to the Hospital . The Accountant shall be responsible for the operation of the hospital account, which shall be under the supervision of the Medical Officer in charge. (4) There shall be in every council Hospital a strong room and a safe for custody of money of the Hospital . (5) The Medical Officer in charge shall initiate and originate all payments regarding Hospital activities. (6) The Council Hospital account shall be audited in accordance with the Local Government Financial memorandum and regulations hereof; (7) There shall be signatories for every check of Hospital government committee the Medical Officer in charge or hospital secretaries or supportive service in charge shall be category "A" and Council Director or treasury shall be category "B".

provisions.title: Relation with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital Governing Committee shall, in performance of its Functions; (a) make sure that the community needs are addressed; (b) solicit funds from various sources; (c) keep and maintain a Health Funds contributor's register; (d) develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration coordination and integration with other service in the area; (e) make sure that the Hospital management team is represented in the Hospital Governing Committee; (f) make sure that the Hospital management team is represented in the Municipal Health Governing committee.

provisions.title: Relationship of Hospital Governing Committee with other committee or authorities
provisions.content: The Hospital governing committee shall for the purposes of better management Liase with other health committees or providers for the purposes fo creating an integrated Health service delivery system in the council.

provisions.title: Reporting and accountability
provisions.content: The Hospital Governing Committee is accountable to the Council through Municipal Health Service Board .

provisions.title: Establishment of Council Hospital Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established in the Municipal the Council Hospital Management Team.

provisions.title: Composition of the Council Hospital Management Team
provisions.content: The Council Hospital Management Team shall consist of:— (a) Medical Office in-charge ; (b) Hospital Secretary; (c) Supportive Services in-charge; (d) Clinical Service in-charge; (e) Outpatient in-charge; (f) In patient in-charge.

provisions.title: Establishment of the Health Center Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established for each Health Center a Health center governing Committee which shall be composed of: (a) 3 members selected from service area wards of which at least one shall be a female; (b) One member appointed by the council from the dispensary committee; (c) One member appointed by private for profit Health facility in the area; (d) the officer in-charge of the health Center who shall be the secretary; (e) One member from the ward development committee; (f) One member appointed by private not for profit health facility. (2) Health Center Governing committee may invite other persons who are not members of the Health Center committee to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take a part in the final decision of the committee. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women.

provisions.title: Qualification of membership
provisions.content: A Person shall qualify for appointment as a member of the Health Centre Governing Committee if he satisfies the following conditions: (a) He is a citizen of United Republic of Tanzania; (b) He has attained the age of twenty five years & above and of sound mind; (c) He is ordinarily resident within the area; (d) The level of his education is primary school or above and can read and write in Kiswahili; (e) He does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) The term of office of a member shall be three years and all the members shall retire on the third anniversary of the date on which they assumed their officers, and if not re-appointed for another term of three years, newly appointed members shall come in office on that day shall fill their places. (2) Any appointed member may resign by giving a written notice of one month to the Chairman; (3) Where a member resigns or dies the vacant post shall be filled and the appointed member shall hold office only for the remainder of the term of his predecessor but shall be eligible for re-appointment. (4) If the Health Centre Committee fails to fulfill its expected obligations the council shall reprimand or dissolve the committee and arrange for an immediate selection; (5) The list of elected candidate shall be forwarded to ward development committee for final selection after being scrutinized by ward management team.

provisions.title: Meetings of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Governing Committee shall hold ordinary meeting for the transactions of its business at least once in every three months at a place, date, and such time as it may decide provided that Committee may convene an extra ordinary meeting as may deem fit. (2) The Chairman of the Committee may convene a special meeting of the Committee after he receives a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of the members of the committee stating clearly the purposes for which the special meeting is to be convened. (3) The secretary shall not less than 24 hours before the time appointed for the holding of a meeting of a Governing Committee notify every member in writing of the place, day and time of the meeting and of the business proposed be to transact at the meeting. (4) The chairman shall be elected among the elected members.

provisions.title: Functions of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of every Committee of a Health Centre :— (a) to receive and discuss implementation reports prepared by the Health Centre management Team ; (b) to liaise with the Council Health Board and other partners in health provision and promotion in its area; (c) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply of drugs and logistic system; (d) to advise and recommend to the Council Health Board on human resources development in terms of recruitment; training, deployment and motivation at the Health Center level; (e) to facilitate the Health Centre Management Team in planning and managing community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (f) to promote the availability of adequate Health centre infrastructure; (g) to oversee the availability of essential supplies of drugs reagents and medical equipment; (2) The Health Center Governing Committee shall be responsible and Accountable to the Council Health Board .

provisions.title: Funds and source of Revenue
provisions.content: (1) Funds and sources of revenue of a Health Centre shall constitute of the following:— (a) Community contributions; (b) Donors or partner donations; (c) Governments grants; (d) Municipal Council grants; (e) Any other source legally acquired. (2) There shall be a special account for a Health Centre in the City at a bank recognized by the Council where all the funds for a Health Cen­tre shall be deposited. (3) There shall be two signatories for a cheque of a Health Centre . The Officer in-charge of a Health Centre or the MMOH or the health secretary shall be category “A” and Municipal Director or Treasury shall be category “B”. (4) There shall be in every Health Centre a strong room and a safe for the custody of money. (5) The Health Centre account shall be audited in accordance with the Local Government Financial memorandum and regulations. (6) The Health Centre shall send quarterly, biannually and annual progress reports to the Council Health Board to be presented before the full Council .

provisions.title: Relation with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Health Centre Governing Committee shall, in performance of its Functions: (a) make sure that the community needs are addressed; (b) solicit funds from various sources; (c) Keep and maintain a Health Fund contributor’s register (d) develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration, coordination and integration with other sectors in the area; (e) make sure that the Dispensary Governing Committee is represented in the Health Centre Committee where circumstances allow; and (f) make sure that the Health Centre Governing Committee is represented in the Council Health Service Board . (2) The Council Health Management team shall give technical advice to the Health Centre Management Team.

provisions.title: Establishment of Health Center Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established a Health Centre management team

provisions.title: Establishment of a Dispensary Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established a committee to be known as a Dispensary Governing Committee . (2) A Dispensary Governing Committee shall be composed of the follows:— (a) elected members who shall be vote members as follows:— (i) Three members from community dispensary users; (ii) One representative from a private for profit health facility; (iii) One representative from private not for profit health facility; (b) Secretariat (non voters):— (i) One representative from a Ward Development Committee; (ii) One representative from a Village/Mtaa Government Committee; and (iii) In-charge of the dispensary that shall be the Secretary. (3) A dispensary Governing Committee may invite persons who are not their members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons will not take part in the final decisions of the Governing committee; (4) At least one third of the members shall be women. (5) A chairman of the Dispensary committee shall be elected from amongst its elected members.

provisions.title: Mode of selection and tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) Member of a Dispensary Governing Committee shall be selected based on the following procedure:— (a) Ward Executive Office/shall advertise the vacant post for representation of Health facilities users; (b) The ward executive officer will hold a public meeting where by suitable candidates will be elected; (c) the list of elected candidates from an opening meeting shall be forwarded to the Ward Management Team for scrutiny before being forwarded to Ward Development Committee which shall do the final selection; and (d) The final selection shall then be forwarded to the Council for approval and inform accordingly the Ward Development Committee. (2) Tenure of office for Dispensary Governing Committee members shall be three years but may seek re-election for another term of three years.

provisions.title: Meetings of Dispensary Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Governing Committee shall meet once in every three months but may convene an extra-ordinary meeting when a need arise. (2) Fifty percent of the elected members together with a Chairperson or Secretary shall form a quorum for any meeting of a Dispensary Governing Committee .

provisions.title: Qualification of members
provisions.content: A Person shall be eligible to be a member of a Dispensary Governing Committee if:— (a) He has attained the age of twenty five years and above, and of sound mind; (b) He has completed primary education or above and knows how to read and write Kiswahili; (c) He is Tanzania and ordinarily a resident of that particular area. The condition of citizenship shall not apply to a person who is working in a voluntary health facility; (d) He is representing the users of a dispensary, not a council employee and he is not holding any political elected post through a political party; (e) He has not been convicted of any criminal offence.

provisions.title: Functions of Dispensary Governing Committee
provisions.content: A Dispensary Governing Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to ensure that the population in its area of jurisdiction receives appropriate and affordable health care services; (b) to receive, discuss and pass the dispensary plans and budget; (c) to receive the implementation report prepared by Dispensary Management Team; (d) to identify and solicit financial resources for running dispensary services; (e) to liaise with other health boards and partners in health provisions and promotion; (f) promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply and logistic system; (g) to advise and recommend on human resources concerning re­cruitment training selection and deployment to relevant authorities; (h) to assist and facilitate the Dispensary Management Team in planning and managing the community based health initiatives within is catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (i) submit quarterly, biannual and annual reports to the Council Health Board .

provisions.title: Relationship of Dispensary Governing Committee with relevant Stakeholders
provisions.content: For the better performance and efficiency in its day to day activities; a Dispensary Governing committee shall liaise with the following:— (a) Community to ensure that community needs are adequately addressed and facilitate flow of information from both ways; (b) Council Health Management Team which shall give technical advice to it; (c) the Council Health Service Board by sending reports; (d) the Dispensary management Team to ensure that the team implements Council ’s decisions; (e) the Health Center Governing Committee by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (f) Other stakeholders by soliciting funds develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration co-ordination and integration.

provisions.title: Source of funds
provisions.content: Source of funds for a Dispensary Governing Committee shall include but not limited to the following:— (1). Community contribution; (a) Council funds; (b) Funds from donors or partners; (c) Central Government; and (d) Other sources legally acquired. 2. The shall be a special account for a dispensary in the Municipal at a bank recognized by the Council where all the funds for a Dispensary shall be deposited. 3. The shall be two signatories for a cheque of a Dispensary . The Clinical Officer in charge of a Dispensary , his deputy or a Nurse shall be category “A”, and the chairperson of the dispensary governing commit­tee and one other member within the committee shall be category “B”. 4. There shall be in every Dispensary a strong room and a safe for custodian of money for Dispensary . 5. Dispensary account shall be audited in accordance with the Local Government Financial regulations. 6. The Dispensary shall send quarterly, biannual and annual progress reports to the Council meetings.

provisions.title: Establishment of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established Ward Health Committees.

provisions.title: Composition of the Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: The ward Health Committee shall be composed of: (a) the councilor of the respective ward; (b) ward Executive Officer; (c) one Head teacher from the primary school located in the respec­tive Ward who shall be appointed by the Ward development com­mittee; (d) two members from the community elected by the community of which one shall be a female; (e) clinical Officer in-charge of health care facility who shall be the Secretary of the committee s or health officer or health assistant where there is no dispensary but he or she will not vote; (f) one member appointed by ward development committee from amongst person proposed by the Mtaa within the area of the Ward; (g) one representative from a community based organization appointed by the ward Development committee; (h) ward Health officer/Assistant. (2) A person shall be eligible to be a member of a ward health com­mittee if: (a) he is a citizen of United Republic of Tanzania; (b) he has attained the age of twenty five years & above and of sound mind; (c) he has a primary education and can read and write Kiswahili; (d) he does not hold any political post. (3) Tenure of officer and mode of selection of the Ward health com­mittee shall be as in the Health center committee, by involving respec­tive organs of the ward. (4) At least one third of the member shall be women. (5) Chairman of the Ward health committee shall be elected from amongst the members.

provisions.title: Functions of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: Ward Health Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to monitor progress of implementation of Health Plan; (b) to initiate and coordinate Community Health Plans; (c) to control epidemics and outbreaks; (d) to organize general meetings of members of the Community and (e) to mobilize community to participate in Ward MTAA/Village environment health committee.

provisions.title: Source of funds
provisions.content: Source of fund for a Ward Health Committee shall be: (a) community contributions; (b) donors and partners donations; (c) council’s grants; and (d) any other source legally acquired.
